title: The 11 Best Salon & Spa Management Software
subtitle: A ranked analysis of the top platforms for managing appointments, clients, payments, and marketing for modern salons and spas.
audience: Salon and spa owners managing appointments and clients

editor.name: Top 11 Editorial
editor.credential: Autonomous AI ranking engine — methodology v1.0 weights public
editor.conflict_disclosure: None. The editor of Top 11 is not a candidate on this list.

independence.statement: Top 11 takes no payment from any provider on this list. Scores are computed from a public weighted rubric; methodology weights were locked before entry research began.

changelog.text: Initial publication. Methodology v1.0 weights Core Scheduling (30%), Client Management (25%), POS & Payments (20%), Ease of Use (15%), and Integrations (10%).

answer_capsule: The best salon and spa management software is Square Appointments for its seamless payment integration, followed by Vagaro for its powerful marketplace and Fresha for its robust free plan.

criteria.name: Core Scheduling & Booking
criteria.description: Effectiveness of the appointment calendar, online booking portal for clients, resource management, and automated reminders.

criteria.name: Client Management & Marketing
criteria.description: Depth of CRM features, client history tracking, and built-in tools for email/SMS marketing, loyalty programs, and reviews.

criteria.name: POS & Payments
criteria.description: Integration of payment processing, inventory management for retail products, commission tracking, and reporting capabilities.

criteria.name: Ease of Use & UI/UX
criteria.description: Intuitive design for both staff and clients, speed of the platform, and quality of mobile applications.

criteria.name: Integrations & Ecosystem
criteria.description: Ability to connect with third-party software like accounting (QuickBooks), marketing (Mailchimp), and other business tools.

guide.q: What's the most important factor when choosing salon software?
guide.a: The core appointment book. If it's not fast, reliable, and easy for your staff to use every minute of the day, no other feature matters. Test the calendar functionality extensively during your free trial.

guide.q: Should I choose a free or paid plan?
guide.a: Start with a free plan (like Fresha's) if you're a new solo operator. However, most growing salons will quickly need the marketing, reporting, and advanced inventory features that only come with paid plans, which typically start around $25-$50 per month.

how_to_choose: Identify your must-have features (e.g., integrated payments, SMS marketing, detailed reporting) before you start your search.
Sign up for free trials from your top 2-3 choices and run mock scenarios, like booking a complex appointment or processing a retail sale.
Check the payment processing rates. A low monthly software fee can be offset by high transaction fees, so calculate the total cost.
Read recent user reviews focusing on customer support quality, as you'll inevitably need help during setup or troubleshooting.

faqs.q: What is the best free salon and spa software?
faqs.a: Fresha is widely regarded as the best free salon software. Its free plan includes unlimited appointments, locations, and staff members, with revenue generated through optional payment processing and advanced feature subscriptions.

faqs.q: How much does salon software typically cost?
faqs.a: Salon software costs range from free to over $300 per month. A typical small to mid-sized salon can expect to pay between $30 and $150 per month, depending on the number of staff members and required features like advanced marketing or inventory controls.

faqs.q: What are the essential features of salon management software?
faqs.a: Essential features include an intuitive appointment calendar, online client booking, automated reminders (SMS/email) to reduce no-shows, a client database (CRM), integrated point-of-sale (POS) for services and products, and basic sales reporting.

faqs.q: Can I manage product inventory and retail with this software?
faqs.a: Yes, most comprehensive salon management platforms include robust inventory management. This allows you to track stock levels, set low-stock alerts, manage purchase orders, and sell retail products through the integrated POS system.

honest_disclosures: Most providers are US-centric, though many offer global support and currency options. Non-US users should verify local payment gateway compatibility.
Pricing is often tiered by the number of 'bookable staff,' which can significantly increase costs as your team grows.
The term 'free' often means the core scheduling is free, but payment processing fees and charges for advanced features apply.

glossary.definition: The system used to process transactions for services and retail products. In salon software, this is integrated with the appointment book and client profiles.

entries.name: Square Appointments
entries.best_for: Salons of all sizes that want a seamless, all-in-one solution for booking, payments, and point-of-sale from a trusted provider.
entries.verdict: Square Appointments is the best overall salon software because it flawlessly integrates a user-friendly booking system with a best-in-class payment and hardware ecosystem, making it incredibly simple for any salon to manage its entire business from one place.
entries.verdict_short: Flawlessly integrates booking and payments within a trusted, easy-to-use ecosystem.
entries.praise: The hardware and software integration is unmatched, providing a reliable and professional checkout experience for clients.
entries.criticism: Advanced marketing and client loyalty features are less robust than some dedicated salon platforms.
entries.criticism_short: Marketing and loyalty features are basic.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Vagaro
entries.best_for: Growing salons and spas that want to leverage a client-facing marketplace to attract new business alongside a feature-rich management platform.
entries.verdict: Vagaro earns its high rank with a comprehensive feature set and a unique marketplace that actively helps salons attract new clients, making it more than just a management tool but a genuine growth engine.
entries.verdict_short: A true growth engine with its client marketplace and extensive feature set.
entries.praise: The built-in marketplace is a significant advantage for discovering new local customers who are actively looking for services.
entries.praise_short: Excellent marketplace for new client discovery.
entries.criticism: The interface can feel cluttered and overwhelming at times due to the sheer number of features available.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Fresha
entries.best_for: New or budget-conscious salons and independent professionals who need a powerful, free booking system and are willing to pay for advanced features as they grow.
entries.verdict: Fresha is the best free option on the market, offering unlimited scheduling and staff accounts at no monthly cost, making it an unbeatable starting point for new businesses before they need more advanced marketing tools.
entries.verdict_short: Unbeatable free core platform, ideal for new salons and independent stylists.
entries.praise: The free tier is incredibly generous, providing core functionality that competitors charge significant monthly fees for.
entries.criticism: The business model relies on payment processing fees and add-ons, which can become costly as transaction volume increases.
entries.criticism_short: Costs can add up with add-ons.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Mindbody
entries.best_for: Larger, established wellness businesses, spas, and multi-location salons that require deep, enterprise-level features for marketing, reporting, and class scheduling.
entries.verdict: Mindbody is a powerhouse for large-scale operations, offering an unparalleled depth of features for marketing automation, complex scheduling, and business analytics that justify its premium price for businesses that can leverage its full potential.
entries.verdict_short: A feature-rich powerhouse for large, complex wellness and beauty businesses.
entries.praise: Its marketing automation and client retention tools are among the most sophisticated in the industry.
entries.criticism: The platform can be complex, expensive, and overwhelming for smaller salons or solo operators.
entries.criticism_short: Complex and expensive for small businesses.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: GlossGenius
entries.best_for: Independent stylists and modern, brand-conscious salons that prioritize a beautiful, streamlined client booking experience and stylish marketing tools.
entries.verdict: GlossGenius stands out by focusing on aesthetics and simplicity, providing the most elegant and user-friendly client-facing interface, which helps solo entrepreneurs and small salons build a premium brand identity.
entries.verdict_short: Delivers an elegant, brand-focused experience for both stylists and clients.
entries.praise: The client booking website and automated email/SMS communications are beautifully designed and highly professional.
entries.criticism: It lacks the deep inventory management and advanced reporting features needed by larger, more complex salons.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Boulevard
entries.best_for: High-end salons and medspas focused on delivering a premium, white-glove client experience with self-booking and communication.
entries.verdict: Boulevard justifies its premium pricing by delivering a meticulously designed platform focused on optimizing the client journey, from intuitive self-booking to seamless checkout, making it ideal for luxury brands.
entries.verdict_short: A premium platform designed for a high-end, client-centric experience.
entries.praise: The platform's design and workflow are exceptionally clean and modern, reducing friction for both staff and clients.
entries.criticism: The higher price point makes it inaccessible for smaller salons or those not focused on the luxury market segment.
entries.criticism_short: High price point for smaller salons.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Zenoti
entries.best_for: Large, multi-location salon and spa chains that require centralized management, advanced analytics, and enterprise-grade features.
entries.verdict: Zenoti is the undisputed leader for enterprise-level salon and spa chains, offering powerful tools for managing multiple locations from a single dashboard, with deep business intelligence and employee management capabilities.
entries.verdict_short: The leading choice for enterprise chains needing centralized control and analytics.
entries.praise: Its ability to manage complex operations across dozens or hundreds of locations is unparalleled in the market.
entries.criticism: The system is far too complex and expensive for single-location salons or small businesses.
entries.criticism_short: Overkill and too costly for small salons.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Mangomint
entries.best_for: Salons and spas looking for a modern, fast, and exceptionally well-designed interface with powerful automation features.
entries.verdict: Mangomint's primary strength is its thoughtfully designed, lightning-fast user interface, which significantly reduces staff training time and makes daily operations smoother than many of its more cluttered competitors.
entries.verdict_short: A beautifully designed, fast interface that streamlines daily salon operations.
entries.praise: The user experience is superb, with a clean, uncluttered layout and intuitive workflows that are a pleasure to use.
entries.criticism: It is on the pricier side for its feature set, and its marketing tools are not as developed as some rivals.
entries.criticism_short: Pricey for its feature set; limited marketing.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Booker by Mindbody
entries.best_for: Established, service-based businesses like spas and salons that need a reliable, feature-complete system and can benefit from the Mindbody ecosystem.
entries.verdict: Booker is a veteran platform that offers a robust and reliable set of core features for salon and spa management, making it a safe choice for businesses that prioritize stability over cutting-edge design.
entries.verdict_short: A stable and reliable veteran platform with a comprehensive feature set.
entries.praise: The platform is mature and stable, with a deep feature set that has been refined over many years.
entries.criticism: The user interface feels dated compared to more modern competitors, and being part of the larger Mindbody ecosystem can sometimes be confusing.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Rosy Salon Software
entries.best_for: Salons that want a straightforward, no-frills management platform with strong customer support and features designed by industry professionals.
entries.verdict: Rosy provides a solid, dependable software solution with all the essential tools a salon needs to operate, backed by a reputation for excellent, responsive customer support.
entries.verdict_short: A solid, dependable solution with all the essentials and great customer support.
entries.praise: Customer support is frequently cited as a major strength, with a helpful team that understands the salon industry.
entries.criticism: The user interface and overall design lag behind more modern, cloud-native competitors.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Acuity Scheduling
entries.best_for: Tech-savvy solo practitioners or small studios that need a powerful, highly customizable scheduler and prioritize integrations over salon-specific features.
entries.verdict: Acuity Scheduling is a wildcard because it's not a true salon software, but a best-in-class general appointment scheduler that can be adapted for salon use, offering unparalleled flexibility and integration power for those who don't need built-in POS or deep inventory management.
entries.verdict_short: A powerful general scheduler offering flexibility for those who don't need salon-specific POS.
entries.praise: Its scheduling engine is incredibly robust, with granular controls for availability, appointment types, and intake forms.
entries.praise_short: Incredibly robust and flexible scheduling engine.
entries.criticism: It completely lacks salon-specific features like retail inventory management, commission tracking, and a dedicated POS interface.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.
